posts.text:
Beijing’s oil import strike continues: 

Chinese companies sold West Africa crude on Thursday, with several Nigerian and Angolan grades hitting the widest discount in at least ~15 years to the Dated Brent benchmark. 

If China doesn’t show up very, very soon (or Iran doesn’t up
posts.summary: Chinese oil demand has collapsed to the point where West African crude oils are trading at their widest discounts to Brent in approximately 15 years, signaling a severe shortage of buyers in the world's largest oil importer. The market faces potential supply imbalances if China's demand doesn't recover quickly or if Iranian crude doesn't increase to fill the gap.

posts.text: Tune in later for an event with @SecretaryWright at @INL 👀 https://t.co/uiea31Eh7l
posts.summary: The Department of Energy is hosting an event with Secretary Wright at Idaho National Laboratory, though the post provides no details about the agenda or potential oil market implications.

posts.text: The sale nets Iran not a few million as JD Vance suggested, but ~$3 billion https://t.co/PV9ft5FjoJ
posts.summary: Patrick De Haan noted that Iran's oil sale generates approximately $3 billion in revenue, significantly more than the few million dollars previously suggested by JD Vance. The statement highlights the substantial financial scale of Iranian oil transactions in the market.

posts.text:
Hormuz traffic sees a sharp d/d uptick

Confirmed Strait of Hormuz crossings rose to 70 on 24 June, up 105% day on day, as demining efforts advanced and operators increasingly used the Omani route. Commercial traffic accounted for most activity, with 53 transits, while low-risk https://t.co/Afhj0gqoHt
posts.summary: Strait of Hormuz crossings surged 105% day-on-day to 70 on June 24, driven by demining progress and increased use of the Omani route, suggesting improved passage conditions for oil tankers through this critical chokepoint. The sharp uptick in commercial traffic indicates reduced disruption risk to global crude flows in the near term.

posts.text:
🚨@SecretaryWright on @POTUS’ American Nuclear Renaissance:

"President Trump came in with aggressive goals and an aggressive plan, a thoughtful plan, to restart the American nuclear industry!” https://t.co/u43Tf9CkHp
posts.summary: The Department of Energy highlighted the Trump administration's plan to revitalize the U.S. nuclear industry through aggressive and strategic initiatives. While nuclear energy development could reduce long-term oil demand for electricity generation, the post provides no specific details on oil market impacts or timelines.

posts.text:
Saudi Arabia appears to be about re-starting oil loadings inside the Persian Gulf. 

Three Saudi-owned in ballast VLCCs are concentrating around the Ju’aymah terminal (it has 6 single point moorings).

If the loadings go ahead, it would be the first known since early March.
posts.summary: Saudi Arabia may be resuming oil loadings at its Ju'aymah terminal in the Persian Gulf for the first time since early March, with three empty tankers positioning at the facility. The resumption could signal a shift in Saudi oil export activity after a months-long pause.

posts.text:
US diesel crack spreads (i.e., refining margins, NYH ULSD vs Brent) are ripping higher again today.

Up ~$12/bbl over the past three days. https://t.co/7SxEjqCHbT
posts.summary: US diesel refining margins have surged approximately $12 per barrel over three days, indicating increased profitability for refiners producing ultra-low sulfur diesel at New York Harbor relative to Brent crude prices.

posts.text: BREAKING: GasBuddy Projects Second Most Expensive July 4 at the Pump Despite Six Weeks of Declines… National average price of gasoline on July 4 projected at $3.75/gal; on track for second most expensive Independence Day at the pump ever
posts.summary: GasBuddy projects the national average gasoline price will reach $3.75 per gallon on July 4, marking the second most expensive Independence Day at the pump on record despite six weeks of price declines. The projection suggests sustained upward pressure on fuel costs heading into the holiday period.

posts.text:
Iran, paraphraed: 

nice ship, shame if something were to happen to it https://t.co/EZv832rXqT
posts.summary: Iran has allegedly made a veiled threat toward a ship, which could potentially disrupt maritime commerce and create supply concerns in global oil markets. The implied threat to vessel safety in key shipping routes may pressure oil prices upward if incidents occur.

posts.text:
IMF says working with Venezuelan authorities as they assess needs after earthquakes
WASHINGTON, June 25 (Reuters) - The International Monetary Fund said it was monitoring developments in Venezuela after two powerful earthquakes hit the country, with thousands feared dead,
posts.summary: The IMF is coordinating with Venezuelan authorities following severe earthquakes that have caused significant casualties, though the article provides no specific information about potential impacts on Venezuela's oil production or markets.

posts.text:
SpaceX plans to build 'Starpipe' natural gas pipeline to fuel Starship rockets

SpaceX plans to begin next month building an eight‑mile (13-km) natural gas pipeline called "Starpipe" to its Texas launch facilities, according to county filings, as Elon ​Musk’s company seeks to https://t.co/NryhJAR6hZ
posts.summary: SpaceX is planning to construct an eight-mile natural gas pipeline to its Texas launch facilities to fuel Starship rockets, beginning next month. The pipeline project could increase natural gas demand in the region to support the company's expanded launch operations.

posts.text:
*SPACEX PLANS TO BUILD NATURAL GAS PIPELINE TO FUEL ROCKETS:RTRS 

!!
posts.summary: SpaceX is planning to build a natural gas pipeline to fuel its rockets, which could increase demand for natural gas as an energy source. This development may have implications for natural gas markets and pricing dynamics.

posts.text: Did we reach peak Fed hawkishness on Monday?  Since then, the US 2-year Treasury yield has fallen from a 16-month high of 4.23% to around 4.10%. While still trading above the current Fed funds rate, the move suggests investors are beginning to question whether additional https://t.co/RHtENPEvzl
posts.summary: Following the Fed's recent hawkish stance, the 2-year Treasury yield has declined from 4.23% to 4.10%, suggesting investors may be doubting the necessity of further interest rate increases. This potential shift toward looser monetary policy could support oil prices, as lower rates typically reduce borrowing costs and boost economic activity and energy demand.

posts.text:
Iran tells ships in Strait of Hormuz to turn back
At least four vessels change course as Tehran challenges International Maritime Organization’s evacuation route https://t.co/QsfHO28btY
posts.summary: Iran is directing ships to turn back in the Strait of Hormuz and challenging the International Maritime Organization's evacuation route, with at least four vessels already changing course. This action could disrupt shipping traffic through one of the world's critical oil transit chokepoints.

posts.text: With no apparent change in the underlying supply and demand outlook, #copper's latest setback appears to be primarily a technically driven correction, amplified by a stronger US dollar and broad-based risk reduction. The move has forced hedge funds and CTAs to reduce a net long https://t.co/1UAxlreCfb
posts.summary: The text discusses copper market dynamics rather than oil market implications, focusing on technical corrections and fund liquidations in copper driven by dollar strength and risk reduction. No oil market implications are addressed in the provided content.

posts.summary: West Texas Intermediate (WTI) crude oil has fallen back below the $70 per barrel mark, indicating a downward price movement in the oil market.

posts.text: U.S. PETROLEUM inventories depleted by another 10 million barrels last week and have fallen by a total of 151 million barrels since the United States and Israel attacked Iran at the end of February. Total stocks of crude and refined products (including in the Strategic Petroleum https://t.co/DAzGbYxYd3
posts.summary: U.S. petroleum inventories fell by 10 million barrels last week and have declined 151 million barrels total since late February, reflecting significant drawdowns in crude and refined product stocks including Strategic Petroleum Reserve holdings. The substantial inventory depletion suggests tightening supply conditions in the U.S. oil market.

posts.text: Thousands feared dead in Venezuela after two major earthquakes https://t.co/NbTZ0mfoPs
posts.summary: Two major earthquakes in Venezuela could potentially disrupt the country's oil production and infrastructure, given that Venezuela holds the world's largest proven oil reserves. The extent of oil market impact would depend on damage to production facilities and export capabilities in the affected regions.

posts.text:
Global primary energy demand is set to rise by 23% to 2050.

Global primary energy demand is set to increase from 312 million barrels of oil equivalent per day (mboe/d) in 2025 to nearly 383 mboe/d in 2050. This is an increase of around 23% over the outlook period, or 0.8% p.a. https://t.co/zPZs7aDTYE
posts.summary: Global energy demand is projected to grow 23% by 2050, rising from 312 to 383 million barrels of oil equivalent per day, indicating sustained long-term demand for petroleum and related energy sources.

posts.text:
Iran’s Persian Gulf Strait Authority (PGSA) has issued a stark warning to international maritime traffic, stating that any vessels transiting the Strait of Hormuz outside of its officially designated routes will no longer be guaranteed safe passage.
#oott https://t.co/ormJZIpzTH.
posts.summary: Iran's Persian Gulf Strait Authority has warned that vessels deviating from designated shipping routes through the Strait of Hormuz will not be guaranteed safe passage, potentially creating navigational risks for oil tankers transiting one of the world's most critical energy chokepoints.

posts.text:
As the effects of the energy crisis ripple across the globe, strengthening efficiency progress can offer key benefits for energy security & affordability.

Decision-makers will discuss this opportunity at next week's Global Energy Efficiency Conference 👉 https://t.co/GinMIFak0z https://t.co/YIDHRaAeuY
posts.summary: The IEA highlights that improving energy efficiency can help address global energy security and affordability challenges stemming from the energy crisis, with policymakers set to explore this topic at an upcoming conference.

posts.text: Oil price back at prewar levels as Gulf flows pick up https://t.co/LkAZbKueEY
posts.summary: Oil prices have declined to levels seen before the war, driven by increased crude flows from the Gulf region. The rise in supply availability is contributing to downward pressure on global oil markets.

posts.text:
Rapid action by Gulf producers has helped keep oil flowing to global markets amid the Hormuz crisis – with the expanded use of alternate shipping routes sustaining exports.

And recent developments point to a recovery in transit through the Strait 👉 https://t.co/TCQftkW355 https://t.co/xqkcbtoDud
posts.summary: Gulf oil producers have mitigated supply disruptions from the Hormuz crisis by shifting to alternative shipping routes, with signs indicating a gradual return to normal transit through the strait.

posts.text: As anyone who drives a car that requires premium gasoline will surely tell you, the large difference between the prices of regular and premium grades shows no signs of going away. Today, we look at how the two were once priced, when they diverged, and what it likely means for https://t.co/hhGeI49mdd
posts.summary: The post examines the historical pricing relationship between regular and premium gasoline, indicating a persistent and widening price gap that has market implications worth analyzing.

posts.text: Fire at 200,000 b/d Monroe Energy refinery in Trainer, PA.  May explain the strong bid for RBOB and gasoline today. https://t.co/oCHJOCvHf8
posts.summary: A fire at the Monroe Energy refinery in Trainer, PA, which processes 200,000 barrels per day, likely contributed to increased demand and pricing for RBOB gasoline in the market today. The incident reduces regional refining capacity and could tighten gasoline supply.

posts.text: You'll see a lot of stories about gasoline demand destruction in coming days. I think they miss the mark. Read my analysis as to why: https://t.co/f7ZiOEu92m
posts.summary: Tom Kloza suggests that forthcoming reports of gasoline demand destruction may be misleading or incomplete in their analysis of current market conditions. He directs readers to his full analysis for his reasoning on why this assessment is flawed.

posts.summary: Copper demand is rising due to AI-driven data center expansion, which has limited direct implications for the oil market but underscores how energy-intensive digital infrastructure is driving broader commodity demand across sectors.

posts.text:
Zhejiang's Blue Whale Bioenergy obtains China SAF export quota

▪️Company receives 305,000 mt annual quota
▪️China now has six authorized SAF exporters

https://t.co/NOBXtYiibk https://t.co/ghBxT0jbMn
posts.summary: Zhejiang's Blue Whale Bioenergy has received a 305,000 metric ton annual quota for sustainable aviation fuel (SAF) exports, bringing China's total authorized SAF exporters to six. This development expands China's capacity to supply the growing global demand for sustainable aviation fuel alternatives to conventional jet fuel.

posts.text:
Norway's Var Energi pulls trigger on trio of hydrocarbon discoveries

▪️FID taken on Cerisa, Ofelia, Gjoa Nord finds
▪️Development to extend Gjoa hub life to 2040
▪️Supports Var's 400,000 boe/d long-term goal

https://t.co/47XLp5mHE2 https://t.co/of56ZJZndN
posts.summary: Var Energi has made final investment decisions on three Norwegian hydrocarbon discoveries (Cerisa, Ofelia, and Gjoa Nord) that will extend production from the Gjoa hub through 2040 and support the company's target of 400,000 barrels of oil equivalent per day.

posts.text:
Europe has a narrow window to rebuild gas storage before winter — and it is running behind 

EU storage stood at 46% of capacity as of 23 June, roughly 15 bcm below the five-year average. At the rate injections have been running, the end-October fill would reach approximately 75% https://t.co/HhsjbmKpqM
posts.summary: Europe's below-average gas storage levels heading into winter could increase energy costs and potentially boost oil demand as countries seek alternative energy sources to compensate for gas shortages. Current injection rates suggest storage will fall short of adequate winter reserves, creating supply risks that may support energy market prices broadly.

posts.text: U.S. propane inventories continued their seasonal climb last week, reaching 90 MMbbl—24% above year-ago levels and 13% above the previous 5-year maximum. Meanwhile, U.S. propane exports remained elevated at 2.03 MMb/d, above both recent averages and 2025 levels.
posts.summary: U.S. propane inventories have surged to historically high levels at 90 MMbbl—significantly above year-ago and five-year average levels—while export demand remains robust at 2.03 MMb/d, indicating strong global demand offsetting domestic oversupply conditions. The combination of elevated inventory buildup and sustained export activity suggests stable market fundamentals despite ample domestic supply availability.

posts.text:

▶️ https://t.co/liZ32yjpAB #naturalgas https://t.co/hKeCTpP29Y
posts.summary: The EIA has released its latest weekly natural gas storage report and supplement, providing updated data on U.S. natural gas inventory levels. This report is relevant to energy markets as natural gas prices can influence crude oil dynamics through their effects on overall energy demand and production costs.

posts.text: Oil Futures Pick Up After Dip To Pre-War Levels - Barron's - https://t.co/bWM5U8upok #GoogleAlerts
posts.summary: Oil futures have rebounded after dropping to pre-war price levels, according to reporting shared by Phil Flynn. The recovery suggests a potential stabilization in the oil market following the recent dip.

posts.text: Answer: Our Garyville refinery has maintained OSHA’s Voluntary Protection Program (VPP) Star status for more than 30 years. It’s the longest-running VPP Star designation of any refinery in the U.S. First achieved in 1994, this recognition highlights exceptional safety practices
posts.summary: Marathon Petroleum's Garyville refinery has maintained OSHA's Voluntary Protection Program Star status for over 30 years, the longest such designation among U.S. refineries, reflecting strong operational safety standards at a major production facility.
